channel.title: Naija Digest Online
channel.description: Nigeria’s one-stop hub for breaking news, tech, football, celebrity gossip, Naija music, live TV, exchange rates and trending topics.

item.title: Two dead, 20 injured in Portugal bus crash
item.dc:creator: Punch
item.description:
Tragedy strikes near Lisbon as a Portugal bus crash leaves two dead and 20 injured. Prime Minister expresses dismay and offers condolences.

item.description: "Mere refereeing errors or irregularities, even if they are numerous or repeated over time, do not constitute, on their own, sufficient evidence of the existence of the scheme alleged by the applicants," said the judgement. item.title: Oil Gains As Focus Shifts To Demand, Supply Recovery
item.dc:creator: Channels TV
item.description: Brent crude futures gained 85 cents, or 1.2%, to $72.84 a barrel, while U.S. West Texas Intermediate crude rose 74 cents, or 1.1%, to $69.29 a barrel as of 0645 GMT, after settling down at around pre-Iran war levels on Monday. item.title: Climate-smart apartments designed to beat the heat
item.dc:creator: France 24
item.description: While most of France suffered from last week's heatwave, a certain number of Montpellier residents were in total comfort in their apartments – without air conditioning. From Middle Eastern-inspired latticework maximising ventilation to curved facades acting as giant sunshades, everything has been d

item.title: Russian threat, Greenland: Denmark’s struggle to tackle fake news
item.dc:creator: France 24
item.description: Faced with US pressure over Greenland and Russia’s belligerence in the East, Denmark finds itself caught between twin threats - and at the nexus of disinformation campaigns. With online campaigns dividing public opinion on crucial issues, the race is on to push back against fake news.

item.dc:creator: Bloomberg Markets
item.description: Angola plans to raise as much as $320 million selling shares in Unitel SA, the country’s biggest telecommunications operator, to help finance its budget and deepen the oil-rich nation’s capital markets.

item.title: Lagos and Singapore were once equals—Here is why one still floods
item.dc:creator: Nairametrics
item.description: On the morning of June 29, 2026, the Lekki-Epe Expressway disappeared. Four hours of relentless rain submerged one of Lagos’s principal arteries under a sheet of water that vehicles could not navigate and many could not avoid. The Apapa-Oshodi Expressway flooded. Ikorodu Road flooded. Sections

item.title: Microsoft fixes storage-hogging Windows 11 folder
item.dc:creator: The Verge
item.description: Microsoft is addressing a Windows 11 bug that caused a folder to take up several gigabytes of storage space. As spotted earlier by Windows Latest, Microsoft included the patch in its optional June 2026 update (KB5095093), which "improves disk space usage for the CapabilityAccessManager.db-wal file."

item.dc:creator: Leadership
item.description: Paris FC have appointed Englishman Liam Rosenior as their new head coach on a three-year contract that runs until June 2028. item.description: The Senate has warned that Nigeria may review its diplomatic relationship with South Africa if renewed xenophobic attacks against Nigerians continue. The

item.dc:creator: Bloomberg Markets
item.description: Apollo Global Management Inc.’s Torsten Slok warned that companies outside the giants of the technology sector are failing to show gains in profitability from their spending on artificial intelligence, posing risks to Big Tech valuations.

item.dc:creator: Leadership
item.description: Paraguayan Senator Celeste Amarilla has warned that she could take legal action against France captain Kylian Mbappé after he accused her of making racist remarks following France’s victory over Paraguay at the 2026 FIFA World Cup. The dispute stems from France’s 1-0 Round of 16 victory

item.title: Britain's Farage resigns as MP to stand for re-election amid furore over gift worth millions
item.dc:creator: France 24
item.description: Nigel ​Farage, ​the leader of populist party ‌Reform ⁠UK, ‌said on Tuesday ⁠he was resigning as an MP to stand for re-election so that the public could decide whether he should be ousted from parliament over a scandal involving the alleged non-disclosure of gifts with millions of pounds.

item.title: NPO Lauds Tinubu Over Planned Investigation Of Big Tech, AI Platforms’ Content Exploitation
item.dc:creator: Leadership
item.description: The Nigerian Press Organisation (NPO), has applauded President Bola Tinubu’s directives to the Federal Competition and Consumer Protection Commission (FCCPC) to formally investigate major global technology companies and generative Artificial Intelligence (AI) platforms operating in Nigeria. NP

item.title: Nigeria adopts DNA technology to track crop varieties, targets stronger seed system
item.dc:creator: Tribune
item.description: The National Agricultural Seeds Council (NASC) has announced plans to institutionalise DNA-based crop variety monitoring as part of efforts to strengthen Nigeria’s seed system, improve food security and ensure farmers have access to genuine improved seed varieties. The announcement was made on

item.title: 2026 budget: Atiku queries alleged diversion of funds for road projects
item.dc:creator: Tribune
item.description: Former Vice President Atiku Abubakar has accused President Bola Ahmed Tinubu of either being complicit in or completely unaware of alleged budget manipulations and the Presidential Foreign Intervention Promotion Council (PFIPC) scandal, saying the administration’s handling of the matter has deepened

item.title: Umahi Seeks Stakeholder Collaboration To Address Flooding Along Lagos-Calabar Coastal Highway
item.dc:creator: TVC News
item.description: Minister of Works, David Umahi, has called for greater collaboration among stakeholders to address concerns over flooding in communities along the Lagos-Calabar Coastal Highway corridor. item.title: Fuel price may reduce as OPEC+ agrees to increase oil production
item.dc:creator: Tribune
item.description: The world's largest alliance of oil-producing countries, OPEC+, has agreed to increase oil production by another 188,000 barrels per day from The post Fuel price may reduce as OPEC+ agrees to increase oil production appeared first on Tribune Online.

item.title: Tinubu tackling insecurity, First Lady assures Qatari envoy
item.dc:creator: Vanguard
item.description: “Talking about evil, evil does not have a face. It comes in different forms, and what we are experiencing in Nigeria is terrorism and kidnapping. The post Tinubu tackling insecurity, First Lady assures Qatari envoy appeared first on Vanguard News.

item.title: Wahala App thinks public safety deserves its own app
item.dc:creator: TechCabal
item.description: Wahala App is betting that the same instinct that sends Nigerians to X during emergencies can power a platform built specifically for public incident reporting.

item.title: IPOB condemns midnight raid, abduction of Nnamdi Kanu’s doctor
item.dc:creator: Tribune
item.description: The Indigenous People of Biafra (IPOB) has alleged that the personal physician to its leader, Nnamdi Kanu, Emeritus Prof. Martin Aghaji, was The post IPOB condemns midnight raid, abduction of Nnamdi Kanu’s doctor appeared first on Tribune Online.

item.title: Brentford To Sign England Striker Wilson
item.dc:creator: Leadership
item.description: Brentford are set to sign former England striker Callum Wilson on an initial 12-month contract. The 34-year-old forward, who left West Ham at the end of last season, is in advanced talks with the west London club, with the deal expected to be completed this week. Wilson is a free agent following his

item.title: DMO July Savings Bond Interest Offer Highest In 2026, At 15.716%
item.dc:creator: Channels TV
item.description: The subscription window runs from July 6 to July 10, 2026, with settlement scheduled for July 15, providing retail investors access to low-risk, government-backed securities. The post DMO July Savings Bond Interest Offer Highest In 2026, At 15.716% appeared first on Channels Television.

item.title: SpaceX’s two lead underwriters have a $1 trillion chasm between their valuations as IPO quiet period ends
item.dc:creator: MarketWatch
item.description: The two lead underwriters on SpaceX’s initial public offering, Goldman Sachs and Morgan Stanley, have a valuation gap of more than $1 trillion as they both initiated coverage at buy.

item.title: Child Abuse: Foundation Commends Abia Police For Swift Arrest Of 35-Year-Old Woman
item.dc:creator: New Telegraph
item.description: The Onyedinma Foundation has commended the Abia State Police Command for the swift arrest and planned prosecution of Mrs. Nwoko Gift, a 35-year-old suspect captured in a viral video allegedly attempting to harm a 12-year-old girl, Miss Ebo Chimamanda, with a kitchen knife in Umuahia, Abia State. New

item.title: Backlog of NOUN Law graduates demand admission into Nigerian Law School, threaten nationwide protest
item.dc:creator: ICIR Nigeria
item.description: GRADUATES of the National Open University of Nigeria (NOUN) Law programme have renewed calls for their admission into the Nigerian Law School, alleging that more than 4,000 qualified graduates have been denied entry into the mandatory professional training programme since 2020. Addressing journalist

item.title: Babachir Faults SGF For Failing To Flag Alleged Fake PFIPC
item.dc:creator: Leadership
item.description: Former Secretary to the Government of the Federation (SGF), Babachir David Lawal, has faulted the Office of the Secretary to the Government of the Federation for allegedly failing to detect and stop documents relating to the purported Presidential Foreign Investment Promotion Council (PFIPC),
